issues.description: ZeroDivisionError on b == 0. No guard, no fallback. Common runtime crash for any caller passing 0 (e.g., computing an average of zero items, or a default-initialized denominator).
issues.fix: Either raise a domain-specific exception (e.g., ValueError('divisor must be non-zero')) or return a sentinel; document the contract.

issues.description: SQL injection. user_id is interpolated directly into the SQL string via f-string. Any caller passing attacker-controlled input can read or destroy arbitrary data (UNION-based exfiltration, stacked DROP TABLE, etc.). Classic, fully exploitable.
issues.repro:
read_user("0 OR 1=1")  ->  returns every user row.
read_user("0; DROP TABLE users; --")  ->  drops the table (if driver permits stacked statements).
issues.fix: Use a parameterized query: db.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = %s", (user_id,)). Cast/validate user_id to int at the boundary.

issues.description: Unvalidated int() conversion. Raises ValueError on non-numeric or empty/whitespace strings, and TypeError on None. No upper/lower bound check, so negative ages and absurdly large ages pass through silently (e.g., parse_age('-7'), parse_age('999999999')).
issues.repro:
parse_age('')      -> ValueError
parse_age(None)    -> TypeError
parse_age('-7')    -> returns -7
parse_age('1e9')   -> ValueError (actually fine, but '999999999999999999' returns int)
issues.fix: Wrap in try/except returning None or raising a typed error; reject ages outside [0, 150].

summary_first_500: Two MAJOR issues: divide_numbers crashes on b==0 with no guard, and read_user is a textbook SQL injection via f-string interpolation. One MINOR: parse_age has no error handling or range validation. Verdict REPAIR.
